message:
beads: close cmkuk + r16hf now that their fixes have landed

Flips two tracker records from in_progress -> closed:

  - flywheel_connectors-cmkuk  fcp-discord: invalid embeds input
                                silently discarded in send/edit paths.
                                Closed: fixed in d8572f12 — parse_embeds
                                helper maps serde_json errors to
                                FcpError::InvalidRequest { code: 1003 }
                                naming the embeds field, and is wired
                                into both invoke_send_message and
                                invoke_edit_message in place of the
                                silent .ok() discard. Covered by two
                                regression tests
                                (send_message_malformed_embeds_returns_typed_error,
                                edit_message_malformed_embeds_returns_typed_error)
                                that exercise string-vs-array and
                                array-of-primitives-vs-array-of-objects
                                on both paths. fcp-discord suite now
                                63/63 (61 prior + 2 new).

  - flywheel_connectors-r16hf  qdrant: real-Qdrant testcontainer
                                lifecycle coverage. Closed: fixed in
                                658cd606 (initial suite) + 9df6f9fb
                                (feature gate + upsert race fix).

The close records include the landing SHA + comment text so the
tracker is self-contained when read standalone (no need to cross-ref
git log for why each issue was closed).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1138,7 +1138,7 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-cdj","title":"fcp.1password: Credential Management Connector","description":"## Overview
Secure credential management connector for 1Password integration, enabling AI agents to safely access and manage secrets without exposing sensitive data.

## Core Capabilities

### Item Lookup (Read-Only by Default)
- Search vault items by name, tags, or categories
- Retrieve specific fields (username, URL, notes) without exposing passwords
- Read-only access as the default permission level
- Explicit elevation required for password retrieval

### Password Generation
- Generate strong passwords with configurable requirements
- Support for length, character sets, and complexity rules
- Memorable password generation (word-based)
- PIN generation for numeric-only contexts

### Entry Creation
- Create new login entries with generated passwords
- Support for secure notes, credit cards, identities
- Custom field support for application-specific data
- Automatic categorization and tagging

### Vault Organization
- List and navigate vault structure
- Move items between vaults
- Bulk tagging operations
- Archive and restore items

### Secure Note Access
- Read secure notes for configuration data
- Structured note parsing (YAML, JSON embedded)
- Document attachment retrieval
- Version history access

## Security Requirements

### Credential Protection
- **CRITICAL**: Never expose credentials in logs or output
- Mask all sensitive fields in debug output
- Use secure memory handling
- Clear credentials from memory after use

### Time-Limited Credential Leases
- Short-lived access tokens (5-minute default)
- Automatic credential rotation
- Lease renewal with re-authentication
- Audit trail for all credential access

## Implementation Notes
- Use 1Password CLI (op) or Connect API
- Support both personal and team accounts
- Service account integration for automation
- MFA handling for interactive sessions

## Testing Requirements
- Mock vault for unit tests
- Integration tests with test vault
- Security audit for credential handling
- Penetration testing for leak prevention","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:21:48.804778945Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-15T07:34:56.145167808Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T07:34:56.145167808Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of flywheel_connectors-8egw","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-cga","title":"fcp.spotify: Podcast Episode Data Access","description":"# fcp.spotify: Podcast Episode Data Access (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide access to podcast metadata for discovery and summarization workflows.

## Scope
- Get show metadata.
- List show episodes (pagination).
- Get episode metadata.
- Optional: saved episodes list (ties to library mgmt).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `spotify.podcasts.read`

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Treat titles/descriptions as tainted external content.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `spotify.show.get`
  - `spotify.show.episodes.list`
  - `spotify.episode.get`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - pagination
  - market handling

## Acceptance criteria
- Podcast metadata access is reliable and correctly typed.

All with optional market parameter and pagination. Spotify at 42 ops total, 113 tests pass, golden vectors verified.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:40:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ciy20","title":"[FCP3/P4.5] Add host backed integration coverage across native and WASI execution forms","description":"Background: the platform must preserve one execution model across multiple execution forms. That claim needs host backed tests that exercise native and WASI sandboxed connector execution against the same semantics and produce enough detail to explain mismatches.

Scope: add integration coverage and scriptable end to end harnesses that prove the host, SDK, and kernel contract behaves consistently across local native and sandboxed WASI execution forms, including lifecycle, checkpoint, health, and evidence surfaces. Require structured lifecycle logs and artifact capture so a failure can be traced to a specific stage of the runtime model.

Acceptance: execution form neutrality is backed by comprehensive tests and detailed logs rather than by assumption.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:06.817941Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-01T19:02:30.456770Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T19:02:30.456461Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","integration","logging","mesh-first","phase-4","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ciy20","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-6t9s0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:36.112373Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2243,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ciy20","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-01: Added execution form neutrality integration tests in crates/fcp-host/tests/no_mock_integration.rs:

1. execution_form_health_vocabulary_is_unified: Verifies HealthState (Starting/Ready/Degraded/Error/Stopping) serializes deterministically across execution forms
2. execution_form_self_check_vocabulary_is_unified: Verifies SelfCheckReport (Ok/Degraded/Failed) serde roundtrip is form-neutral
3. execution_form_doctor_report_schema_stable: Verifies DoctorReport schema (status/version/zone_id/checkpoint/revocation/audit) is consistent
4. execution_form_connector_health_variants_cover_both_forms: Verifies ConnectorHealth (Healthy/Degraded/Unavailable) covers both native and WASI failure modes

Combined with existing coverage: 43+ no_mock_integration tests cover doctor, self_check, health, budget lifecycle, and discovery across the host. Telegram connector (306 tests) and Discord connector (524 tests) prove the E2E contract works for native execution. WASI sandbox has 543+ tests in fcp-sandbox. Together these prove execution form neutrality through the shared vocabulary.","created_at":"2026-04-01T19:02:25Z"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-cmkuk","title":"fcp-discord: invalid embeds input is silently discarded in send/edit paths","description":"## Finding
Both `invoke_send_message()` and `invoke_edit_message()` parse `input["embeds"]` with `serde_json::from_value(...).ok()` ([connectors/discord/src/connector.rs:1338-1346](connectors/discord/src/connector.rs:1338), [1448-1455](connectors/discord/src/connector.rs:1448)).

Because the deserialization error is discarded, malformed embed payloads are treated as `None` instead of producing a typed request error. In `send_message`, that falls through to the generic `Either `content` or `embeds` must be provided` branch; in `edit_message`, malformed embeds bypass embed validation entirely and the edit proceeds as though no embeds were supplied.

## Why this matters
Agents get misleading validation feedback and can accidentally send/edit messages after supplying malformed embeds. This is a silent input-shape downgrade, not strict request validation.

## Recommendation
Decode `embeds` with `map_err(...)` and return `FcpError::InvalidRequest { code: 1003/1004, message: ... }` that explicitly says the embeds payload is malformed.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:56:43.535144Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T16:08:52.282269Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-cmkuk","title":"fcp-discord: invalid embeds input is silently discarded in send/edit paths","description":"## Finding
Both `invoke_send_message()` and `invoke_edit_message()` parse `input["embeds"]` with `serde_json::from_value(...).ok()` ([connectors/discord/src/connector.rs:1338-1346](connectors/discord/src/connector.rs:1338), [1448-1455](connectors/discord/src/connector.rs:1448)).

Because the deserialization error is discarded, malformed embed payloads are treated as `None` instead of producing a typed request error. In `send_message`, that falls through to the generic `Either `content` or `embeds` must be provided` branch; in `edit_message`, malformed embeds bypass embed validation entirely and the edit proceeds as though no embeds were supplied.

## Why this matters
Agents get misleading validation feedback and can accidentally send/edit messages after supplying malformed embeds. This is a silent input-shape downgrade, not strict request validation.

## Recommendation
Decode `embeds` with `map_err(...)` and return `FcpError::InvalidRequest { code: 1003/1004, message: ... }` that explicitly says the embeds payload is malformed.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:56:43.535144Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T16:19:45.090339Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T16:19:45.089178Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2815,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cmkuk","author":"jemanuel","text":"Landed in d8572f12. Added parse_embeds() helper that maps serde_json errors to FcpError::InvalidRequest { code: 1003 } naming the embeds field; wired into both invoke_send_message and invoke_edit_message in place of the silent .ok() discard. Two regression tests (send_message_malformed_embeds_returns_typed_error, edit_message_malformed_embeds_returns_typed_error) cover wrong-shape string-vs-array and array-of-primitives-vs-array-of-objects on both paths. fcp-discord: 63/63 tests passing (61 prior + 2 new) via rch on /tmp/fcp-p7-t32.","created_at":"2026-04-21T16:19:44Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3","title":"fcp.datadog: Datadog Observability Platform Connector","description":"# fcp.datadog - Datadog Observability Platform Connector (FCP2)

## Strategic Context

### Value Assessment
**Strategic Value Score: 84/100** — Important Tier 2 connector (Observability family).

**Why Critical**: Observability is essential for autonomous operations. Agents that can understand system health, diagnose issues, and correlate incidents enable truly autonomous SRE workflows. Datadog is the **market leader in cloud observability**.

### Priority Matrix Position
- **Category**: Strategic Investment (Specialized + High Value)
- **Build Phase**: Phase 2 Productivity (weeks 5-8)

---

## V2 Specification Compliance (NORMATIVE)

### ConnectorArchetype
```rust
archetypes: vec\![
    ConnectorArchetype::Knowledge,    // Metrics, logs, traces queries
    ConnectorArchetype::Operational,  // Monitor management, incidents
    ConnectorArchetype::Streaming,    // Webhook events
]
```
- **Knowledge**: Query metrics, logs, traces, dashboards
- **Operational**: Monitor CRUD, incident management
- **Streaming**: Webhooks for alerts, incidents

### ConnectorStateModel
```rust
state_model: ConnectorStateModel::Stateless
```
- **Stateless**: Query operations are independent
- Time-range based queries (no cursor)
- Monitor state managed by Datadog

### ConnectorFormat
```rust
format: ConnectorFormat::Wasi
```
- **WASI recommended**: Handles API credentials
- Memory isolation for key protection
- Capability-gated hostcalls

### NetworkConstraints (NORMATIVE)
```rust
NetworkConstraints {
    // Datadog API endpoints (region-specific)
    host_allow: vec\![
        "api.datadoghq.com".into(),       // US1
        "api.datadoghq.eu".into(),        // EU1
        "api.us3.datadoghq.com".into(),   // US3
        "api.us5.datadoghq.com".into(),   // US5
        "api.ap1.datadoghq.com".into(),   // AP1
    ],
    port_allow: vec\![443],
    deny_localhost: true,
    deny_private_ranges: true,
    deny_tailnet_ranges: true,
    deny_ip_literals: true,
    require_sni: true,
    require_host_canonicalization: true,
}
```

### TaintFlags (NORMATIVE)
```rust
// Telemetry data is internal/trusted
metric_data.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::NONE;

// Log messages may contain user data
log_message.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::EXTERNAL_INPUT;

// Webhook payloads are external
webhook_event.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::EXTERNAL_INPUT;
```

### SafetyTier per Operation
| Operation | SafetyTier | Rationale |
|-----------|------------|-----------|
| `datadog.metrics.query` | Safe | Read-only |
| `datadog.logs.search` | Safe | Read-only |
| `datadog.traces.search` | Safe | Read-only |
| `datadog.dashboards.read` | Safe | Read-only |
| `datadog.monitors.read` | Safe | Read-only |
| `datadog.monitors.write` | Dangerous | Alert configuration |
| `datadog.incidents.manage` | Dangerous | Incident lifecycle |
| `datadog.dashboards.write` | Risky | Dashboard changes |

---

## Agent Capabilities (Concrete Use Cases)

### Metrics & Monitoring
- Query metrics (Datadog query language)
- Access dashboards
- Alert management
- Anomaly detection

### Logging
- Log search and analysis
- Log pattern detection
- Error correlation

### APM & Tracing
- Distributed trace inspection
- Service dependency mapping
- Performance bottleneck identification

### Incident Management
- View and manage incidents
- Correlate with metrics/logs
- Postmortem data

### Examples of Agent Workflows
```
"Show me CPU usage for the past hour"
"Find error logs from the checkout service"
"What traces show latency > 500ms?"
"List all triggered monitors"
"Create a monitor for high error rate"
"Get incident timeline"
```

---

## Flywheel Ecosystem Synergies

### Sentry Integration
**Unified error tracking:**
- Error correlation
- Performance impact analysis

### GitHub Integration
- Deploy events → metric correlation
- Release impact analysis

### Slack/Twilio Integration
- Alert escalation
- Incident notifications

### Linear/Jira Integration
- Incident → issue creation
- Performance issue tracking

---

## Technical Implementation Notes

### API Surface
- **REST API v1/v2**: Primary interface
- **Webhooks**: Alert and incident events

### Recommended Crates
- `reqwest` — HTTP client
- `serde_json` — JSON handling

### Authentication
```rust
// API Key + Application Key
DD-API-KEY: {api_key}
DD-APPLICATION-KEY: {app_key}
```

### Query Language
```
avg:system.cpu.user{env:production} by {host}
```

---

## Zone Architecture

### Recommended Zones
| Zone | Description | Capabilities |
|------|-------------|--------------|
| `z:ops:datadog:full` | Full access | All operations |
| `z:ops:datadog:read` | Read-only | Queries only |
| `z:ops:datadog:monitors` | Monitors | Alert management |

---

## Manifest Archetypes (Closed Set)
- `knowledge` — Telemetry queries
- `operational` — Monitor management
- `streaming` — Webhook events

---

## External Surface

### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only:
- `api.datadoghq.com:443` (region-specific)

### Credential Injection
- API key + App key via egress proxy
- Never persist credentials

---

## Capability Model

### Query Operations
- `datadog.metrics.query` — Metric queries
- `datadog.logs.search` — Log search
- `datadog.traces.search` — Trace search
- `datadog.dashboards.read` — Dashboard data

### Management Operations
- `datadog.monitors.read` — List monitors
- `datadog.monitors.write` — Create/update monitors (Dangerous)
- `datadog.incidents.manage` — Incident lifecycle (Dangerous)

### Admin Operations
- `datadog.dashboards.write` — Dashboard changes

---

## Operations (Representative)

### Query Metrics
```json
POST /api/v1/query
{
  "from": 1609459200,
  "to": 1609545600,
  "query": "avg:system.cpu.user{env:production}"
}
```

### Search Logs
```json
POST /api/v2/logs/events/search
{
  "filter": {
    "query": "service:checkout status:error",
    "from": "now-1h",
    "to": "now"
  }
}
```

### List Monitors
```
GET /api/v1/monitor
```

### Create Monitor
```json
POST /api/v1/monitor
{
  "name": "High Error Rate",
  "type": "metric alert",
  "query": "avg(last_5m):sum:errors{service:api} > 100",
  "message": "Error rate exceeded threshold"
}
```

---

## Tests

### Unit Tests (Mock-Only)
- Query construction
- Response parsing
- Time range handling
- Error handling

### Integration Tests
- Mock Datadog API responses
- Multi-query scenarios
- Webhook handling

### E2E Compliance
- Validates NetworkConstraints
- Dangerous operation approval flow
- Audit logging

---

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner (static + dynamic)
- No real Datadog API calls in unit tests
- Metric queries work
- Log search works
- Trace search works
- Monitor CRUD works
- All V2 NORMATIVE requirements satisfied","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T17:09:44.802418506Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-05T08:02:16.303257Z","closed_at":"2026-03-05T08:02:16.303238Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2","tier2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.18","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.21.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.26","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.31","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":747,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Datadog connector complete: 8 operations (events.create, events.list, logs.search, metrics.query, metrics.submit, monitors.create, monitors.delete, monitors.list), multi-region support (US1/US3/US5/EU1/AP1), DD-API-KEY+DD-APPLICATION-KEY auth with secretless credential_id fallback. 109 tests (66 unit + 43 integration), clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-05T08:02:10Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.1","title":"fcp.datadog: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)","description":"## Goal
Produce the full FCP2 manifest and capability map for this connector.

This bead is where we translate the connector’s functional surface (operations) into a **mechanically checkable** contract:
- operation schemas + schema IDs
- capability families per operation
- per-operation NetworkConstraints (default deny)
- sandbox profile requirements
- declared state model (stateless / singleton_writer / crdt)

## Required content (must be explicit)
- Operation list is exhaustive and stable for the current phase.
- Capability IDs DO NOT encode hostnames/ports.
- NetworkConstraints are minimal and per-operation.
- Risk tier for each operation (Safe / Dangerous).
- Explicit bounds: timeouts, max response sizes, pagination limits.

## Tests
- Add good/bad manifest examples for the compliance runner.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes static checks in `flywheel_connectors-1n78.21.5`.
- Default-deny is enforced by manifest + capability requirements.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T12:12:35.227968257Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-03-02T03:03:30.264149Z","closed_at":"2026-03-02T03:03:30.264133Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":352,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Datadog manifest complete. 8 ops across 4 cap families (datadog.metrics.read/write, events.read/write, monitors.read/write, logs.read). Host: *.datadoghq.com + *.datadoghq.eu. Dangerous: monitors.delete (interactive). Hash: 0024d3507c4e34caf55ba5bcd24e9f008337912d2a56a484eda4a1919d34bbaa. Test vectors: manifest_datadog_good.toml, manifest_datadog_bad.toml (3 violations).","created_at":"2026-03-02T03:03:22Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.2","title":"fcp.datadog: Provisioning Automation","description":"## Goal
Automate provisioning and configuration with minimal human steps.

This bead owns:
- auth setup (OAuth / API keys / webhook secrets) via `CredentialObject`/`CredentialId` (egress proxy injection preferred)
- endpoint configuration
- `fcp doctor` readiness checks
- deterministic, mockable provisioning flows

## FCP2 requirements
- Secrets never touch disk.
- Provisioning is deterministic and testable.
- Error messages are actionable and do not leak secrets/PII.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No real external calls in unit tests.
- Provisioning emits structured logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T12:12:35.809889760Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-03-09T03:15:47.880447Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T03:15:47.880431Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1128,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: Implemented provisioning automation for fcp.datadog — provisioning_recipe() with auth mode selection + secret prompt/store steps, provisioning_readiness() method with auth_mode/network_ok/base_url checks, enhanced handle_self_check() including provisioning info. 16 new tests (202 total unit), all pass, clippy clean. Commit a0ff0b44.","created_at":"2026-03-09T03:15:47Z"}]}
@@ -2058,7 +2058,7 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qvsq8","title":"[FCP3/P2.5] Add preservation tests and boundary checks for the semantic carve out","description":"Background: semantic relocation is easy to claim and hard to trust unless there is preservation evidence. The carve out needs tests that prove behavior was preserved where intended while ownership boundaries became stricter.

Scope: add comprehensive crate local unit tests, cross crate boundary tests, compile time negative tests, and deterministic fixtures for the kernel, policy, and evidence split. Require detailed fixture logging that shows which semantic edge is under test, which import path or boundary is being protected, and what artifact should be inspected when a regression fires.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qvzvp","title":"[MEDIUM] SymbolAck/DecodeStatus replay: signed transcripts lack recipient binding","description":"SymbolAck::transcript_bytes (fcps.rs:748-763) and DecodeStatus::transcript_bytes (fcps.rs:606-634) both include epoch_id (cross-epoch replay protection) but neither binds the recipient NodeId or a per-request nonce.

Within a single epoch, an attacker who observes a valid signed SymbolAck from B addressed to A can replay it to a third recipient D, provided D also has a pending request for the same object_id from B in the same epoch. On D, handle_symbol_ack (node.rs:1416) calls sent_symbols.remove(&ack.object_id), prematurely cancelling D's outbound transfer. Same vector for DecodeStatus via process_decode_status.

Recommended fix: extend SymbolAck and DecodeStatus to carry a `to: NodeId` field (or a per-request nonce echoed from the original SymbolRequest) and include it in the signing transcript. Bump transcript domain separators to V2 to prevent cross-version replay.

Discovered during code review of fcp-mesh after the Apr 19 swarm closed the four-handler signature-verification gap (81f303c8). The signature-verification work itself is correct; this is a residual transcript-binding gap exposed by that hardening.

Severity: MEDIUM. Requires multi-recipient request fan-out in the same epoch to exploit, and the consequence is transfer cancellation (availability), not data exfiltration.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:06:43.709405Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:18:54.488352Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T07:18:54.488054Z","close_reason":"Bound SymbolAck/DecodeStatus V2 transcripts to recipient node and request nonce","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-r1sl","title":"TEST: Connector SDK Unit Tests (Standard Methods, Typed Schemas, State Model)","description":"# TEST: Connector SDK Unit Tests

## Goal
Validate the connector SDK standard-method surface is correct and ergonomic via a comprehensive test suite (schemas, state, streaming).

## Scope
Comprehensive tests for connector SDK APIs and patterns.

## Test Categories

### Standard Methods
- [ ] invoke() method signature correct
- [ ] simulate() method signature correct (preflight; no side effects)
- [ ] subscribe() method signature correct
- [ ] get_state() method signature correct
- [ ] set_state() method signature correct
- [ ] introspect() returns correct schema

### Simulate / CostEstimate / Availability (Spec-aligned)

#### Core Simulate Tests
- [ ] SimulateRequest/SimulateResponse canonical encoding round-trip
- [ ] `estimate_cost=true` populates `CostEstimate` deterministically when supported
- [ ] `check_availability=true` populates `ResourceAvailability` deterministically when supported
- [ ] missing capability reporting is stable (ordering + content)
- [ ] simulate MUST NOT call write paths in the test connector (mock-enforced)
- [ ] simulate timeouts are enforced and do not leak secrets in error strings/logs

#### Simulate Golden Vectors
```rust
// tests/vectors/sdk/simulate_preflight_allow.cbor
SimulateRequest {
    id: ObjectId::from_hex("sim-001"),
    operation: "email.send",
    input: json\!({"to": "test@example.com", "subject": "Test"}),
    capability_token: Some(valid_token),
    estimate_cost: true,
    check_availability: true,
}
// Expected: would_succeed=true, estimated_cost populated

// tests/vectors/sdk/simulate_preflight_deny_capability.cbor
SimulateRequest {
    id: ObjectId::from_hex("sim-002"),
    operation: "email.send",
    input: json\!({"to": "test@example.com"}),
    capability_token: None,  // Missing
    estimate_cost: false,
    check_availability: false,
}
// Expected: would_succeed=false, missing_capabilities=["email.send"]

// tests/vectors/sdk/simulate_preflight_deny_policy.cbor
SimulateRequest {
    id: ObjectId::from_hex("sim-003"),
    operation: "storage.write",
    input: json\!({"path": "/sensitive/secrets.txt"}),
    capability_token: Some(valid_token),
    estimate_cost: false,
    check_availability: false,
}
// Expected: would_succeed=false, failure_reason="POLICY_DENIED: path_restricted"
```

#### CostEstimate Scenarios
```rust
#[test]
fn test_cost_estimate_deterministic() {
    // Given the same inputs, cost estimate must be identical
    let req = SimulateRequest { estimate_cost: true, ... };
    let r1 = connector.simulate(req.clone()).await?;
    let r2 = connector.simulate(req).await?;
    assert_eq\!(r1.estimated_cost, r2.estimated_cost);
}

#[test]
fn test_cost_estimate_no_pricing_tables() {
    // CostEstimate must NOT contain CurrencyCost unless connector
    // has explicit, stable currency basis
    let resp = connector.simulate(req).await?;
    if let Some(cost) = resp.estimated_cost {
        // api_credits, estimated_duration_ms, estimated_bytes are OK
        // currency must be None or explicitly stable
        assert\!(cost.currency.is_none() || cost.currency_is_stable);
    }
}

#[test]
fn test_availability_check_rate_limit_headroom() {
    // ResourceAvailability must include rate limit signals when available
    let req = SimulateRequest { check_availability: true, ... };
    let resp = connector.simulate(req).await?;
    if let Some(avail) = resp.availability {
        // Should report remaining/reset if the connector tracks rate limits
        assert\!(avail.rate_limit_remaining.is_some() || avail.no_rate_limit);
    }
}
```

#### Mock Enforcement Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_simulate_never_calls_write() {
    // Mock connector that panics if write paths are called
    let mock = MockConnector::new()
        .on_write(|| panic\!("simulate called write path\!"));
    let req = SimulateRequest { operation: "db.insert", ... };
    // This must NOT panic
    let resp = mock.simulate(req).await?;
    assert\!(\!resp.would_succeed || resp.is_dry_run);
}

#[test]
fn test_simulate_timeout_no_secret_leak() {
    // Ensure timeout errors do not include request data
    let req = SimulateRequest {
        input: json\!({"secret_api_key": "sk-12345"}),
        ...
    };
    let err = connector.simulate_with_timeout(req, Duration::from_millis(1))
        .await
        .unwrap_err();
    assert\!(\!err.to_string().contains("sk-12345"));
    assert\!(\!format\!("{:?}", err).contains("sk-12345"));
}
```

### Typed Schemas
- [ ] Operation schema generation
- [ ] Event schema generation
- [ ] State schema generation
- [ ] Schema validation at runtime
- [ ] Schema evolution rules (V2-only; fail-closed + explicit migration)
  - unknown schema hashes rejected deterministically
  - interface_hash changes are detectable and surfaced via introspection
  - migrations (if any) are explicit and produce new content-addressed objects

#### Schema Golden Vectors
```rust
// tests/vectors/sdk/operation_schema_v1.json
{
  "schema_id": "urn:fcp:schema:email.send:v1",
  "schema_hash": "blake3:abc123...",
  "input": {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "to": {"type": "string", "format": "email"},
      "subject": {"type": "string", "maxLength": 998},
      "body": {"type": "string"}
    },
    "required": ["to", "subject"]
  },
  "output": {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "message_id": {"type": "string"}
    }
  }
}

// tests/vectors/sdk/schema_evolution_reject.json
// When interface_hash changes, must be explicit
{
  "old_hash": "blake3:abc123...",
  "new_hash": "blake3:def456...",
  "expected": "SCHEMA_MISMATCH: interface_hash changed, migration required"
}
```

### State Model
- [ ] State read/write round-trip
- [ ] State versioning works
- [ ] State conflict detection
- [ ] State snapshot creation
- [ ] State restore from snapshot

#### State Golden Vectors
```rust
// tests/vectors/sdk/state_snapshot_v1.cbor
StateSnapshot {
    connector_id: "test-connector",
    version: 1,
    data: {"cursor": "abc123", "last_sync": 1705000000},
    created_at: "2025-01-15T00:00:00Z",
    checksum: "blake3:..."
}

#[test]
fn test_state_conflict_detection() {
    // Concurrent writes must detect conflict
    let state_v1 = connector.get_state().await?;
    let state_v1_clone = state_v1.clone();
    
    // Writer 1 updates
    connector.set_state(state_v1.with_data(json\!({"a": 1})), version: 1).await?;
    
    // Writer 2 tries to update from same version - must fail
    let err = connector.set_state(state_v1_clone.with_data(json\!({"b": 2})), version: 1)
        .await
        .unwrap_err();
    assert\!(matches\!(err, SdkError::StateConflict { .. }));
}
```

### Error Handling
- [ ] SDK errors use error taxonomy
- [ ] Errors include ai_recovery_hint
- [ ] Panic recovery in connector code
- [ ] Timeout handling

#### Error Golden Vectors
```rust
// tests/vectors/sdk/error_taxonomy.json
[
  {
    "code": "FCP-4001",
    "category": "capability",
    "message": "Missing required capability: email.send",
    "ai_recovery_hint": "Request email.send capability from zone administrator",
    "retryable": false
  },
  {
    "code": "FCP-5002",
    "category": "transient",
    "message": "Rate limit exceeded",
    "ai_recovery_hint": "Wait {retry_after_ms}ms before retrying",
    "retryable": true,
    "retry_after_ms": 1000
  }
]

#[test]
fn test_panic_recovery() {
    // Connector panics must be caught and converted to errors
    let mock = MockConnector::new()
        .on_invoke(|| panic\!("connector bug\!"));
    let err = mock.invoke(req).await.unwrap_err();
    assert\!(matches\!(err, SdkError::InternalError { .. }));
    assert\!(err.ai_recovery_hint().is_some());
}
```

### Trait Implementations
- [ ] FcpConnector trait defaults work
- [ ] Capability checking helpers
- [ ] Audit event helpers
- [ ] Receipt creation helpers

## Test File Organization
```
crates/fcp-sdk/tests/
├── sdk_standard_methods.rs     # invoke, simulate, subscribe, etc.
├── sdk_simulate_tests.rs       # Detailed simulate/CostEstimate tests
├── sdk_schema_tests.rs         # Schema generation and validation
├── sdk_state_tests.rs          # State model tests
├── sdk_error_tests.rs          # Error handling tests
├── sdk_trait_tests.rs          # FcpConnector trait tests
└── vectors/
    └── sdk/
        ├── operation_schema_v1.json
        ├── event_schema_v1.json
        ├── state_snapshot_v1.cbor
        ├── simulate_preflight_allow.cbor
        ├── simulate_preflight_deny_capability.cbor
        ├── simulate_preflight_deny_policy.cbor
        ├── cost_estimate_deterministic.cbor
        └── error_taxonomy.json
```

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "timestamp": "RFC3339",
  "test_name": "test_sdk_simulate_preflight_allow",
  "phase": "execute|assert|cleanup",
  "correlation_id": "uuid",
  "operation_id": "...",
  "connector_id": "...",
  "simulate_request": {
    "operation": "email.send",
    "estimate_cost": true,
    "check_availability": true
  },
  "simulate_response": {
    "would_succeed": true,
    "estimated_cost": {"api_credits": 1}
  },
  "result": "pass",
  "duration_ms": 5
}
```

## Integration with E2E Harness
These unit tests form the foundation for E2E scenarios. The mock connectors and golden vectors defined here are reused by:
- `flywheel_connectors-1n78.21.4` (E2E Harness)
- `flywheel_connectors-1n78.36.1` (Happy Path E2E)
- `flywheel_connectors-1n78.36.2` (Denial Path E2E)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All SDK methods tested with positive and negative cases
- State model is reliable with conflict detection
- Error handling is consistent and includes ai_recovery_hints
- Simulate/CostEstimate tests verify no side effects
- Golden vectors are canonical and deterministic
- Logging output matches specification

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-r4x01","title":"[tooling] rch artifact retrieval overflows on pathological .beads recovery path depth","description":"Remote `rch exec -- cargo ...` commands in this repo can finish the actual remote compile/test successfully but then fail during artifact retrieval with rsync buffer-overflow errors against deeply nested `.beads/recovery_*` paths.

Observed repeatedly on 2026-03-25 while verifying `flywheel_connectors-0iek1` and auditing telemetry/conformance slices. Representative failure:
- `overflow: xflags=0x207a ... lastname=.beads/recovery_20260313T010949Z_rubyglen/.../.beads`
- `[Receiver] buffer overflow: recv_file_entry (file=flist.c, line=747)`
- `rsync error: error allocating core memory buffers (code 22)`

Notes:
- `.rchignore` already excludes `.beads/`, `**/.beads/`, `.beads/recovery_*`, and related patterns, so the retrieval phase appears to ignore or bypass the same exclusions that the upload phase uses.
- This is a verification/tooling bug, not evidence that the underlying cargo command failed. Some workers still retrieve artifacts successfully, but the failure is frequent enough to undermine trust in remote validation.
- Fixes must avoid deleting files without explicit user permission. Repo-side mitigation, rch-side retrieval filtering, or safe path-depth containment are acceptable; destructive cleanup is not.

Acceptance criteria:
1. `rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-conformance --all-targets` and comparable commands can complete in this repo without artifact-retrieval failure caused by `.beads` path depth.
2. The solution does not require manual destructive cleanup of recovery trees.
3. The verification story is documented so agents can distinguish remote-command success from post-success artifact retrieval failure when it still occurs.","notes":"Repo-side documentation adjusted; remaining remediation depends on local rch upgrade or upstream/tool rollout rather than a code change inside this repo.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:40:40.535598Z","created_by":"VioletSummit","updated_at":"2026-04-19T15:41:25.623099Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T15:41:25.622591Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["beads","rch","tooling","verification"],"comments":[{"id":1995,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-r4x01","author":"VioletSummit","text":"Claimed and starting investigation. Current hypothesis: remote cargo succeeds but rch artifact retrieval walks pathological .beads recovery paths that should have been excluded. Investigating repo config, rch flags/help, and safe non-destructive mitigations.","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:42:41Z"},{"id":1996,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-r4x01","author":"VioletSummit","text":"Investigation update: traced rch source locally in /Users/jemanuel/projects/remote_compilation_helper. Upload merges transfer.exclude_patterns + .rchignore, but artifact retrieval does not reuse the exclude set; it traverses all dirs via --include '*/' and only filters by artifact globs. Also found hook-level hardcoding of remote sync roots to /data/projects in map_sync_root_to_remote_root() and ensure_worker_projects_topology(), so project config cannot currently force a fresh remote workspace path for cargo offload. This makes the bug fundamentally upstream/tooling-side, though repo docs/config can still make the verification story explicit.","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:47:52Z"},{"id":1997,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-r4x01","author":"VioletSummit","text":"Validation result: telemetry verification succeeded end-to-end on worker vmi1227854, including artifact retrieval. Conformance verification on worker vmi1153651 compiled successfully but then failed during artifact retrieval with the same recursive .beads recovery overflow and rsync code 22. Conclusion: the defect persists, is worker-state-specific, and this repo can only document and pin excludes explicitly; the real fix belongs upstream in rch retrieval filtering and/or worker-path handling.","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:50:12Z"},{"id":1998,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-r4x01","author":"VioletSummit","text":"Repo-side follow-up: README now explicitly says that if rch reports remote command exit=0 and only then fails during artifact retrieval, agents should treat that as a tooling or worker-state problem rather than a Cargo failure, preserve the retrieval stderr, and retry on another worker or continue upstream investigation.","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:56:05Z"},{"id":1999,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-r4x01","author":"GentleOak","text":"Additional validation from GentleOak on 2026-03-25:
- Installed client is /Users/jemanuel/.local/bin/rch -> rch 1.0.16.
- Local remote_compilation_helper checkout is ahead of that install: v1.0.16-2-ga5119e2, latest commit a5119e2 ("fix(transfer): apply effective excludes to retrieval rsync before directory include"). So current upstream source already contains retrieval-side exclude reuse in rch/src/transfer.rs.
- Re-ran `rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-conformance --all-targets` on worker vmi1227854. Sync succeeded and remote command exited 101 because the shared worktree currently fails to compile in crates/fcp-mesh/src/degraded.rs (`DegradedTransportError::Encoding` missing). This run did not reach an artifact retrieval overflow.
- Re-ran `rch exec -- cargo test -p fcp-e2e subprocess::tests::drop_reaps_long_running_process --lib -- --exact --nocapture` on worker vmi1153651. That remote command also exited 101, now due to compile errors in crates/fcp-conformance/src/vectors/fcps.rs (`encoded_frame` is a Result<Vec<u8>, FrameError>` but is still compared/len()/decoded as raw bytes). This run also failed before artifact retrieval.
Conclusion: repo-side docs/config mitigation is already landed, but acceptance criterion #1 cannot be re-evaluated on the current shared worktree until these unrelated compile errors are cleared. When the original overflow does occur after `Remote command finished: exit=0`, the remaining suspicion should be installed-client lag and/or stale worker state, not missing repo excludes.","created_at":"2026-03-25T20:04:21Z"},{"id":2000,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-r4x01","author":"PeachPond","text":"PeachPond update:

Reproduced the current failure again in this repo with installed `rch`: remote `cargo check -p fcp-conformance --all-targets` exited 0 on worker `vmi1153651`, then artifact retrieval overflowed in rsync while traversing `.beads/recovery_20260313T010949Z_rubyglen/.../.beads`.

Traced the tooling gap to upstream `rch` at `/Users/jemanuel/projects/remote_compilation_helper/rch/src/transfer.rs`: upload merges `.rchignore`, but retrieval built its own `--include "*/"` rsync filter and ignored retrieval-side excludes.

Patched upstream locally to add `get_retrieval_excludes()` and make retrieval honor only runtime scratch excludes plus project `.rchignore` entries, rather than blindly reusing the full upload exclude set (which would have wrongly hidden artifact roots like `target/` and `dist/`).

Verification completed:
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p rch build_retrieve_command_applies_rchignore_excludes_before_directory_include -- --nocapture` passed in `/Users/jemanuel/projects/remote_compilation_helper`.
- `cargo fmt --check` passed in `/Users/jemanuel/projects/remote_compilation_helper`.
- Direct dry-run rsync against the same failing worker/path with the patched retrieval filter shape completed cleanly with `rsync_exit=0` and no buffer overflow while enumerating 47,160 files from `/data/projects/flywheel_connectors`.

Limitation: the remotely built patched `rch` artifact is Linux, so I could not execute that binary locally on macOS to replace the currently installed `rch` in-place. This bead looks technically solved in upstream source, but the local `rch` installation still needs to be rebuilt/updated from that patched source before `flywheel_connectors` itself will observe the fix.","created_at":"2026-03-25T20:07:15Z"},{"id":2001,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-r4x01","author":"GentleOak","text":"Fresh reproduction on 2026-03-25 after the current fcps.rs fix was present in the synced worktree:
- Command: `rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-conformance --all-targets`
- Worker: `vmi1153651`
- Result: remote compile/check completed successfully (`Finished dev profile`, `Remote command finished: exit=0 in 122154ms`), then artifact retrieval immediately failed with the original recursive `.beads/recovery_20260313T010949Z_rubyglen/.../.beads` overflow / `recv_file_entry` / rsync code 22.
- This confirms acceptance criterion #1 is still unmet on the installed client path, even after the repo-side docs/config mitigation and after the unrelated compile blockers were cleared enough for the command to reach retrieval again.
- Combined with the earlier source inspection (`remote_compilation_helper` checkout at `v1.0.16-2-ga5119e2`, commit `a5119e2 fix(transfer): apply effective excludes to retrieval rsync before directory include`) versus the installed binary (`rch 1.0.16`), the most likely next step is to validate with a newer local `rch` build or otherwise land the retrieval-exclude fix into the actually installed client. Repo excludes alone are not sufficient on this installed version/worker state.","created_at":"2026-03-25T20:08:59Z"},{"id":2004,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-r4x01","author":"jemanuel","text":"Fresh findings on 2026-03-25: conformance validation now succeeds end to end on worker vmi1227854, but deterministic reproduction remains awkward because the documented `RCH_WORKER` env override is not currently wired for selection in `rch exec`. Evidence: `rch` docs still mention `RCH_WORKER`, but `docs/guides/configuration.md` now explicitly lists the override form as documented-but-not-yet-wired, and a dry run with `RCH_WORKER=vmi1153651` selected a different worker instead of honoring the request. This is adjacent tooling debt that makes the retrieval-overflow bug harder to pin to a specific worker/path state.","created_at":"2026-03-25T20:25:58Z"},{"id":2005,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-r4x01","author":"jemanuel","text":"Root-cause update on 2026-03-25: the installed client on this machine is `rch 1.0.16`, while the retrieval-side exclude fix for `.rchignore` lives on upstream `remote_compilation_helper` main at commit `a5119e2` (`fix(transfer): apply effective excludes to retrieval rsync before directory include`), which is two commits past the `v1.0.16` tag. That version gap explains why the current upstream source shows retrieval filtering for `.beads/recovery_*`, but the released client observed in this repo can still behave as if retrieval ignores those excludes. Repo-side docs/comments were updated to reflect the split between `.rchignore`, `.rch/config.toml`, and client-version behavior.","created_at":"2026-03-25T20:28:36Z"},{"id":2006,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-r4x01","author":"GentleOak","text":"Claimed `flywheel_connectors-r4x01` after `bv --robot-triage`/`--robot-next` again ranked it as the only actionable bead. Taking the local installed-client path next: inspect `/Users/jemanuel/projects/remote_compilation_helper`, preserve any in-flight upstream patch there, rebuild/install a newer local `rch` on this machine if feasible, then rerun `rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-conformance --all-targets` in `flywheel_connectors` to see whether the installed-client gap is the remaining blocker. Agent Mail send/handshake attempts are currently hitting transient DB errors, so using this bead comment as the durable coordination update.","created_at":"2026-03-25T22:54:50Z"},{"id":2007,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-r4x01","author":"PeachPond","text":"PeachPond follow-up on 2026-03-25:

Installed-client gap is now fixed locally on this machine. I built a native macOS `rch` from upstream `remote_compilation_helper` commit `03f9f08421c8dfa92b7e7a5c4f742c65daa3a6ec` (the retrieval-exclude split) using an isolated target dir: `env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-native cargo build -p rch --release --offline --locked --target aarch64-apple-darwin`.

Install details:
- Preserved timestamped backups in `~/.local/bin/rch-backups/`.
- A straight in-place overwrite of `~/.local/bin/rch` produced a binary that macOS killed as `SIGKILL (Code Signature Invalid)` even though `codesign --verify` said valid. The same built artifact ran fine from `/tmp` and from `~/.local/bin/rch.fresh`.
- Resolved that by renaming the broken path aside to `~/.local/bin/rch.bad.20260325T190514` and moving a fresh inode into `~/.local/bin/rch`. After that, `rch --help`, `rch status`, and `rch workers probe vmi1153651` all succeeded normally.

Verification state after the upgrade:
- Direct worker/path validation remains good: the exact failing worker/path `ubuntu@38.242.134.66:/data/projects/flywheel_connectors/` still completes a retrieval-shaped dry-run rsync cleanly with no `.beads/recovery_*` overflow.
- End-to-end `rch exec` from this repo is no longer blocked by the old installed-client version, but it still does not reach remote cargo execution reliably in the current topology.
  - Without overrides: `rch exec --json -- cargo test -p fcp-e2e subprocess::tests::drop_reaps_long_running_process --lib -- --exact --nocapture` fail-opens at dependency preflight `RCH-E327` (`Path dependency topology policy failed; move dependencies under /data/projects (or /dp) and retry.`). I aborted the forbidden local fallback immediately.
  - With scoped env overrides `RCH_CANONICAL_PROJECT_ROOT=/Users/jemanuel/projects RCH_ALIAS_PROJECT_ROOT=/dp`: the same command gets past the policy gate, but then fail-opens at dependency preflight `RCH-E326` (`Dependency verification could not determine remote state; inspect sync/SSH logs and retry.`). I again aborted the local fallback immediately.

Net: the local installed `rch` client is now updated enough that the old retrieval filter bug is not the only blocker, but acceptance criterion #1 is still not satisfied end-to-end because dependency preflight is now stopping remote execution before retrieval can be exercised through normal `rch exec` in this repo. The retrieval overflow itself still appears addressed at the exact worker/path level; the remaining blocker has shifted to dependency-preflight behavior.","created_at":"2026-03-25T23:12:28Z"},{"id":2008,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-r4x01","author":"GentleOak","text":"Local progress update: installed a patched macOS-native rch/rchd/rch-wkr from `/Users/jemanuel/projects/remote_compilation_helper` HEAD (`03f9f08`) after ad-hoc re-signing the copied binaries to fix `SIGKILL (Code Signature Invalid)` from taskgated. First rerun of `rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-conformance --all-targets` on the upgraded client selected `vmi1149989` and hit `RCH-E326`, attempting fail-open local Cargo. I aborted that local fallback per `flywheel_connectors` rules. Next step: temporarily disable/deprioritize `vmi1149989` locally and rerun against a healthier worker such as `vmi1153651`, which currently probes cleanly.","created_at":"2026-03-25T23:16:50Z"},{"id":2087,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-r4x01","author":"OliveMink","text":"Additional diagnosis on 2026-03-29: `bv --robot-triage` still points active work back to `flywheel_connectors-ndoro`, so I re-ran a minimal compliant repro from `/Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors` with scoped topology overrides: `env RCH_CANONICAL_PROJECT_ROOT=/Users/jemanuel/projects RCH_ALIAS_PROJECT_ROOT=/dp rch exec -v --json -- cargo check -p fcp-conformance --all-targets`.

Selected worker was `vmi1156319`; it never reached remote Cargo and instead emitted `Dependency planner fail-open on vmi1156319 [RCH-E326]` followed immediately by `[RCH] local (...)`. I interrupted at the first local-build line (`Blocking waiting for file lock on build directory`) to stay compliant with AGENTS.md.

New tooling-level finding: the local source checkout at `/Users/jemanuel/projects/remote_compilation_helper` is now `cb80c59` (`fix(sync): promote nested path dependencies to enclosing Cargo workspace root`), and its `rch/src/hook.rs` only forces local fallback for policy violations (`should_force_local_fallback_for_runtime_fail_open` returns true only for `RCH-E327`). For `RCH-E326`, that source path would proceed with primary-root-only sync instead of dropping to local.

The installed binary actually in use is still `/Users/jemanuel/.local/bin/rch` (timestamp 2026-03-25 19:14, `rch 1.0.16`), and its runtime behavior does not match the current source tree. Net: the remaining blocker is likely installed-client/runtime skew in `rch` plus dependency-planner behavior, not a new `flywheel_connectors` code regression.
","created_at":"2026-03-29T01:45:16Z"},{"id":2091,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-r4x01","author":"OliveMink","text":"Follow-up on the `RCH-E326` tooling thread: a reduced sibling workspace under the allowed project root topology does provide a compliant verification escape hatch even while the main `flywheel_connectors` workspace still fail-opens locally on the installed `rch 1.0.16` client.

Concrete successful run on 2026-03-29:
- disposable workspace: `/Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors_ndoro_conformance_verify`
- copied subset: `fcp-async-core`, `fcp-async-core-macros`, `fcp-cbor`, `fcp-crypto`, `fcp-core`, `fcp-manifest`, `fcp-store`, `fcp-registry`, `fcp-raptorq`, `fcp-telemetry`, `fcp-tailscale`, `fcp-protocol`, `fcp-mesh`, `fcp-conformance`, plus `tests/vectors/manifest`
- copied manifest trimming: reduced root workspace member list; removed copied `fcp-testkit` path dev-deps from `fcp-core`, `fcp-store`, `fcp-raptorq`, `fcp-mesh`, and `fcp-conformance`
- command: `env RCH_CANONICAL_PROJECT_ROOT=/Users/jemanuel/projects RCH_ALIAS_PROJECT_ROOT=/dp rch exec -v --json -- cargo check -p fcp-conformance --test no_mock_integration`
- worker: `vmi1153651`
- result: real remote Cargo execution completed with exit `0`, and artifact retrieval also completed successfully (`3799 files`, `61830 bytes`, total wall time about `295.0s`)

This does not fix the main-workspace planner/client skew, but it proves the failure is not fundamental to the code or to `rch` remote execution in this repo family once the closure presented to the installed client is reduced enough.
","created_at":"2026-03-29T02:07:01Z"},{"id":2740,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-r4x01","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-18 verification and closure:

All 3 acceptance criteria now satisfied with rch 1.0.17 (installed 2026-04-07):

1. PASS: rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-conformance --all-targets completed successfully.
   - Remote command: exit=0 in 101282ms (worker vmi1152480)
   - Artifact retrieval: 6155 files, 2372759 bytes in 30664ms — NO overflow
   - Retrieval loaded 16 patterns from .rchignore (total: 19)

2. Also verified with fcp-cbor (smaller crate):
   - Remote: exit=0 in 13787ms
   - Retrieval: 2171 files, 415580 bytes in 6068ms — clean

3. Root cause confirmed: rch 1.0.16 retrieval phase ignored .rchignore. Fixed in upstream commit a5119e2 (fix(transfer): apply effective excludes to retrieval rsync before directory include), included in rch 1.0.17.

4. No manual cleanup required. No file deletion.

5. rch --version confirms 1.0.17 installed at ~/.local/bin/rch (2026-04-07).","created_at":"2026-04-19T01:08:01Z"},{"id":2761,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-r4x01","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-19 re-verification: All 3 AC remain satisfied. rch 1.0.17 installed (retrieval-exclude fix a5119e2). .rchignore has 16 patterns covering .beads/ recovery paths. AGENTS.md line 751 and README.md Building section document the verification story (remote success vs retrieval failure). fcp-conformance builds cleanly. Re-closing.","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:41:22Z"}]}
